checks.a_analysis: The output follows the source document's structure and text accurately for the elements it identifies. No instances of fabricated headings, body text, or conclusions were found that do not have a basis in the source PDF.
The phone number (984-204-1487) and Conference ID (766 174 851#) provided in the output exactly match the contact information provided in the source PDF header.

The output contains content from both Page 1 and Page 2 of the source document. While specific text within sections is missing (addressed in C5), all high-level agenda tabs (1-13) and main headings are present.

The table on page 1 of the source is split in the JSON into a table (for the initial items and main tab headers) and lists (for sub-items). While the JSON table contains fewer rows than the visual representation in the PDF, this is a result of structural reformatting into lists rather than data loss from the table itself.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The conversion fails to capture substantial specific details within the agenda items. Specifically, in Tab 5 (Consent Agenda), all financial data including Month-End Balances, Disbursements, and Encumbered Funds for the NG 911, CMRS, PSAP, and Grant funds are missing. Additionally, sub-items under Tab 5f (Monthly Reports and Mitchell County Grant) are omitted. On Page 2, sub-item 8a (FY 2021 Proposed PSAP Distribution) is entirely missing. These omissions represent more than 25% of the content within these respective sections.
checks.b_evidence_source: April 2020 Month-End Balance $ 66,897,707; April 2020 Disbursement $ 65,036; [All financial figures in Tab 5 b-e]; i. Monthly Reports per PSAP; ii. Mitchell County Grant Extension Request; a) FY 2021 Proposed PSAP Distribution (Roll Call Vote Required)……….Marsha Tapler
checks.b_evidence_location: Tab 5 (Consent Agenda) on Page 1 and Tab 8 (Funding Committee Report) on Page 2

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text that was successfully converted appears to accurately represent the wording and names from the source document without altering meaning.

checks.a_analysis: The output does not contain any duplicated text blocks. Each item from the agenda is listed once, following the logical flow of the source document.

The heading hierarchy is preserved and logically structured. 'AGENDA' is correctly identified as H1, and 'NORTH CAROLINA 911 BOARD MEETING' is correctly identified as H2. There are no skipped heading levels.

The table structure is significantly corrupted. In the first page table (reading_order 4), the 'Topic' and 'Presenter' columns are merged into single cells (e.g., 'Call to Order...Eric Boyette'). Additionally, the 'Tab' column is only recognized for items 1-6; for the introductory items, the whole row is treated as one cell. Most critically, the second page of the table (items 7-13) is not rendered as a table at all, but converted into an unordered list (reading_order 11), losing the 'Tab/Topic/Presenter' column structure entirely.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source document contains a consistent 3-column table (Tab, Topic, Presenter) spanning two pages.
checks.b_evidence_output: The output merges columns on page 1 (e.g., cell content 'Call to Order...........................................................................................Eric Boyette') and converts the remainder of the table on page 2 into an unordered list (reading_order 11).

checks.a_analysis: The reading order generally follows the source document. Although the top-left logo is moved to the end of the first page content (reading_order 8), this is considered an acceptable change for accessibility optimization.

The nesting within lists is incomplete. Under item 5 (Consent Agenda), the sub-items 5b, 5c, 5d, and 5e have specific financial data (Month-End Balances and Disbursements) in the source which are omitted in the output. Furthermore, the sub-items i and ii under 5f ('Monthly Reports per PSAP' and 'Mitchell County...') are missing from the list item entirely.
checks.b_evidence_source: f) Grant Project Updates per Reports\r\ni. Monthly Reports per PSAP\r\nii. Mitchell County Grant Extension Request
checks.b_evidence_output: f) Grant Project Updates per Reports

checks.a_analysis: No links were injected into the document. Contact information like the phone number is rendered as plain text, matching the source material.

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of the document is significantly altered. The source PDF utilizes a consistent three-column table ('Tab', 'Topic', 'Presenter') for all agenda items across both pages. In the HTML output, this structure is broken: the first six items are in a table with incorrect column alignment, while sub-items (5a-f, 6a-g) and all items from the second page (7-13) are extracted and rendered as simple bulleted lists. This change destroys the visual flow and intended organization of the agenda information.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF maintains a 3-column table layout for items 7 through 13 on page 2.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot renders items 7 through 13 as a bulleted list starting with 'Education Committee Report...' instead of a table.

checks.a_analysis: The table rendering in the HTML is fundamentally broken. Column alignment is incorrect, as the 'Presenter' column is rendered empty while the presenter names (e.g., 'Eric Boyette') are merged into the 'Tab' or 'Topic' cells. Furthermore, there is significant data loss within the rendered table/list; the financial figures (account balances and disbursements) for all sub-items in section 5 are completely missing from the HTML screenshot.
checks.b_evidence_source: Item 5b: 'April 2020 Month-End Balance $ 66,897,707' and Item 1 'Presenter' column containing 'Eric Boyette'.
checks.b_evidence_output: Item 5b appears only as 'b) NG 911 Fund/Account' with no financial data. In the table, Item 1 'Topic' cell contains 'Chair’s Opening Remarks...Eric Boyette' and the 'Presenter' column is empty.
checks.b_evidence_location: Agenda Table, Item 1 and Item 5 sub-list

checks.a_analysis: The 'NC DIT 911 Board' logo is present in the HTML output. It appears twice, matching the frequency of the logo in the source PDF (once per page). While the positioning of the first logo has shifted from the top-left header to a mid-document position, the meaningful image is rendered and visible.
There are no instances of duplicated body content. The repeated logos correctly correspond to the original layout of the two-page source PDF.
